ksas.statement: Knowledge of program management frameworks (PMI, SAFe, Agile) and how they are adapted for data and AI programs that involve technical uncertainty, iterative development, and cross-functional dependencies.
Knowledge of data and AI program risk types, including technical risk, data availability risk, model performance risk, adoption risk, and regulatory compliance risk.
Skill in managing cross-functional data and AI programs, including workstream coordination, dependency management, stakeholder communication, and escalation.
Skill in producing executive-level program status reporting, including milestone tracking, risk registers, budget status, and decision requests.
Develop and maintain program plans for data and AI initiatives, including scope definition, workstream structure, milestone schedule, resource plan, and risk register.
Manage program execution, including workstream coordination, issue resolution, scope change management, and executive status reporting.
Conduct program retrospectives and lessons learned documentation, producing findings that inform program management practice improvement.
Knowledge of change management frameworks (Prosci ADKAR, Kotter) and how to adapt them for data and AI capability deployments with heterogeneous user populations.
Knowledge of adult learning design principles, including learning objectives, instructional design methods, knowledge retention research, and blended learning delivery models.
Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audiences, from executives to operational staff, with role-appropriate content and delivery formats.
Skill in measuring adoption and identifying adoption barriers, including usage analytics review, stakeholder interviews, and friction point diagnosis.
Design, develop, and deliver data and AI training programs for defined organizational audiences, including needs assessment, curriculum development, delivery execution, and effectiveness measurement.
Manage the data and AI adoption program, including stakeholder engagement plans, adoption milestone tracking, barrier identification, and intervention deployment.
Knowledge of data literacy concepts, including reading and interpreting charts, understanding data sources, recognizing misleading visualizations, and applying basic statistical reasoning.

Knowledge of AI literacy concepts, including what AI is and is not, how ML models work at a conceptual level, AI limitations and failure modes, and responsible AI principles for non-technical audiences.
Skill in translating technical data and AI concepts into accessible language and relatable examples for non-technical business audiences without sacrificing accuracy.
Develop and deliver data and AI literacy curriculum for non-technical organizational audiences, including module design, delivery facilitation, and effectiveness assessment.
Knowledge of data pipeline monitoring concepts, including data freshness, volume anomalies, schema changes, job failure patterns, and the alerting systems used to detect each.
Knowledge of data platform access management processes, including access request intake, provisioning workflows, access review cycles, and deprovisioning procedures.
Skill in triaging data pipeline incidents, including failure identification, impact assessment, escalation decision, and status communication to affected consumers.
Monitor data pipeline health dashboards and respond to alerts per defined runbooks, including initial triage, escalation when needed, and incident documentation.
Process data access requests per defined procedures, including request validation, approval routing, provisioning, and access log documentation.
Execute routine data platform maintenance tasks, including storage cleanup, job configuration updates, and operational documentation maintenance.
